name: 细胞器
subject: biology
curriculum_points: 阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动
举例说明细胞各部分结构之间相互联系、协调一致，共同执行细胞的各项生命活动
建构并使用细胞模型，阐明细胞各部分结构通过分工与合作，形成相互协调的有机整体，实现细胞水平的各项生命活动
尝试制作真核细胞的结构模型

deep_snippets.text: ## Table 4.1 4.5 • The Cytoskeleton Cell Component Function Present in Prokaryotes? Present in Animal Cells? Present in Plant Cells? Cytoskeleton Maintains cell’s shape, secures organelles in specific positions, allows cytoplasm and vesicles to move within cell, and enables unicellular organisms to move independently Yes Yes Yes Flagella Cellular locomotion Some Some No, except for some plant sperm cells Cilia Cellular locomotion, movement of particles along plasma membrane's extracellular surface, and filtration Some Some No ## Table 4.1 4.6 Connections between Cells and Cellular Activities

Some cells contain aggregates of macromolecules surrounded by membranes; these are called organelles. Organelles are small structures that exist within cells. Examples of organelles include mitochondria and chloroplasts, which carry out indispensable functions: mitochondria produce energy to power the cell, while chloroplasts enable green plants to utilize the energy in sunlight to make sugars. All living things are made of cells; the cell itself is the smallest fundamental unit of structure and function in living organisms. (This requirement is why viruses are not considered living: they are not made of cells. To make new viruses, they have to invade and hijack the reproductive mechanism of a living cell; only then can they obtain the materials they need to reproduce.) Some organisms consist of a single cell and others are multicellular. Cells are classified as prokaryotic or eukaryotic. Prokaryotes are single-celled or colonial organisms that do not have membranebound nuclei; in contrast, the cells of eukaryotes do have membrane-bound organelles and a membrane-bound nucleus. In larger organisms, cells combine to make tissues, which are groups of similar cells carrying out similar or related functions. Organs are collections of tissues grouped together performing a common function. Organs are present not only in animals but also in plants. An organ system is a higher level of organization that consists of functionally related organs. Mammals have many organ systems. For instance, the circulatory system transports blood through the body and to and from the lungs; it include

(a) (b) Figure 4.8 These figures show the major organelles and other cell components of (a) a typical animal cell and (b) a typical eukaryotic plant cell. The plant cell has a cell wall, chloroplasts, plastids, and a central vacuole—structures not found in animal cells. Most plant cells do not have lysosomes or centrosomes. If the nucleolus were not able to carry out its function, what other cellular organelles would be affected? a. The structure of endoplasmic reticulum would not form. b. The function of lysosomes would be hindered, as hydrolases are formed by nucleolus. c. The free ribosomes and the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which contains ribosomes, would not form. Chapter 4 | Cell Structure

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 细胞器

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物学
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 10
- **章节/领域**: 细胞的结构
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动
> 举例说明细胞各部分结构之间相互联系、协调一致，共同执行细胞的各项生命活动
> 建构并使用细胞模型，阐明细胞各部分结构通过分工与合作，形成相互协调的有机整体，实现细胞水平的各项生命活动
> 尝试制作真核细胞的结构模型

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「细胞器」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「细胞器」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「细胞器」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
2. 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系健康、生态、农业或生物技术案例，讨论「细胞器」与现实生活的关系。
- 通过模型图、显微图或调查资料拓展学生对生命系统的理解。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
细胞器是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

Some cells contain aggregates of macromolecules surrounded by membranes; these are called organelles. Organelles are small structures that exist within cells. Examples of organelles include mitochondria and chloroplasts, which carry out indispensable functions: mitochondria produce energy to power the cell, while chloroplasts enable green plants to utilize the energy in sunlight to make sugars. All living things are made of cells; the cell itself is the smallest fundamental unit of structure and function in living organisms. (This requirement is why viruses are not considered living: they are not made of cells. To make new viruses, they have to invade and hijack the reproductive mechanism of a living cell; only then can they obtain the materials they need to reproduce.) Some organisms consist of a single cell and others are multicellular. Cells are classified as prokaryotic or eukaryotic. Prokaryotes are single-celled or colonial organisms that do not have membranebound nuclei; in contrast, the cells of eukaryotes do have membrane-bound organelles and a membrane-bound nucleus. In larger organisms, cells combine to make tissues, which are groups of similar cells carrying out similar or related functions. Organs are collections of tissues grouped together performing a common function. Organs are present not only in animals but also in plants. An organ system is a higher level of organization that consists of functionally related organs. Mammals have many organ systems. For instance, the circulatory system transports blood through the body and to and from the lungs; it include

The plant cell has a cell wall, chloroplasts, plastids, and a central vacuole—structures not found in animal cells. Most plant cells do not have lysosomes or centrosomes. The function of lysosomes would be hindered, as hydrolases are formed by nucleolus. c. The free ribosomes and the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which contains ribosomes, would not form. ## Chapter 4 Cell Structure 4.1 Studying Cells 4.2 Prokaryotic Cells 4.3 Eukaryotic Cells 4.4 The Endomembrane System and Proteins 4.5 The Cytoskeleton 4.6 Connections between Cells and Cellular Activities Close your eyes and picture a brick wall. What is the wall's basic building block? It is a single brick. Like a brick wall, cells are the building blocks that make up your body. Your body has many kinds of cells, each specialized for a specific purpose. Just as we use a variety of materials to build a home, the human body is constructed from many cell types. For example, epithelial cells protect the body's surface and cover the organs and body cavities within. Bone cells help to support and protect the body. Immune system cells fight invading bacteria. Additionally, blood and blood cells carry nutrients and oxygen throughout the body while removing carbon dioxide. Each of these cell types plays a vital role during the body's growth, development, and day-to-day maintenance. In spite of their enormous variety, however, cells from all organisms—even ones as diverse as bacteria, onion, and human—share certain fundamental characteristics. 4.1 Studying Cells

exercises.stem: 依据课标「细胞器」：阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：举例说明细胞各部分结构之间相互联系、协调一致，共同执行细胞的各项生命活动
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「细胞器」相关的生命现象。
